title: 1.1: Reports that a GET request to /health returns "success" with HTTP 200.
1.2: Reports that requesting / returns an HTTP 302 redirect to /auth/login, confirming that authentication routing is functional.
1.3: Reports that requesting /auth/login returns a fully rendered HTML login page with HTTP status 200 such as visible login UI elements.
1.4: Reports seeing the registration confirmation screen such as the message “An email to validate your email is on its way” after submitting a signup form, confirming activation was triggered.
1.5: States that clicking the activation link triggers the activation route at `/auth/activate?...` confirming activation completes at runtime.
1.6: States that after activation the user is redirected to the dashboard at /dashboard/ such as seeing a success flash on screen confirming login flow continues.
1.7: Reports alias creation by showing a new alias entry listed in the dashboard such as after clicking “Random Alias” confirming alias persistence.
1.8: States inbound email handling by observing handler logs such as the system logging receipt and processing of the incoming message.
1.9: States that the dashboard “Forwarded” counter increases after the inbound email is delivered confirming that forwarding execution updated runtime statistics.
1.11: Identifies background job runner activity through runtime logs such as seeing “Take job …” confirming scheduled work is processed.
1.12: Identifies email handler startup through runtime logs such as “Start mail controller 0.0.0.0 20381” confirming SMTP handling is active.
